title: Fight Aging: Preprint Claims Exceptional Mouse Lifespan Extension via Telomere Transfer Mechanism
description: A bioRxiv preprint reports that CD4+ T cells release telomere-containing vesicles ('Rivers') into the circulation, extending median mouse lifespan by ~17 months with several mice surviving to nearly five years. Fight Aging cautions that the very large effect size demands stronger supporting evidence.

title: Fight Aging: Within Average Decline, Some Older Adults Show Improved Function
description: New data from a 12-year study of 11,000 adults found that ~32% showed improved cognitive abilities and ~28% improved physically. Positive attitudes toward aging were the strongest predictor of who improved.

title: Immorta Bio's SenoVax + Stem Cell Combo Doubles Mouse Lifespan; Accepted at IMMUNOLOGY2026
description: Immorta Bio reported that combining its SenoVax senolytic vaccine with StemCellRevivify therapy doubled lifespan in a doxorubicin-induced aging mouse model, achieving ~84% median lifespan extension. Research accepted for presentation at IMMUNOLOGY2026.

title: Lifespan.io: Scientists Successfully Freeze and Rewarm Mouse Brain Slices
description: Researchers vitrified mouse brain slices and a complete brain with encouraging results: upon rewarming, much of the neuronal structure and activity was preserved, a milestone for cryopreservation research.

title: ARPA-H Awards $144 Million via PROSPR Program to Build Clinical Trials for Aging Interventions
description: ARPA-H's PROSPR program awarded up to $144M to seven teams to lay groundwork for phase 3 trials of healthspan interventions including rapamycin, SGLT2 inhibitors, and semaglutide in healthy people in their 60s.

title: Unilever Ventures Takes Minority Stake in Longevity Nutraceutical Company NOVOS
description: Unilever Ventures invested in NOVOS, whose flagship NOVOS Core targets all 12 hallmarks of aging. In a University of Surrey RCT, NOVOS Core improved flow-mediated dilation by 2.6-2.9 percentage points, far exceeding typical supplement effects.

title: Lifespan.io: European Heart Journal Review of How the Heart Ages
description: A comprehensive review published in European Heart Journal details how the heart ages at the cellular level, including senescence-driven enlargement, mitochondrial dysfunction, and fatty infiltration of the heart's pacemaker.

title: LongX Opens Applications for Xplore 2026 Remote Summer Fellowship in Longevity
description: Longevity Xplorer opens its third annual Xplore Program, a fully remote fellowship placing students and early-career professionals into longevity biotech project work with partner organizations.

title: Top Longevity Startups by Fundraising: Altos Labs Leads at $3B, Sector Totals ~$10B+
description: A comprehensive ranking of longevity startups by total funding shows Altos Labs ($3B), Oura ($1.2B), and Retro Biosciences ($1.2B) at the top. The top 10 companies captured ~67% of all sector funding.

title: 11 Anti-Aging Biotech Companies Leading Longevity in 2026
description: Labiotech profiles the leading anti-aging biotech companies for 2026 including Altos Labs, Life Biosciences, NewLimit, Genflow Biosciences, and Rejuvenate Bio across reprogramming, gene therapy, and senolytic approaches.

title: Fight Aging: ARPA-H Funding Deployed for Clinical Trials Relevant to Aging
description: Fight Aging notes that treating aging as a medical condition is no longer fringe, with ARPA-H program managers deploying sizable public funding alongside private sources to advance aging interventions into clinical trials.

title: Lifespan.io: ATP Deficiency Identified as Novel Mechanism Driving Parkinson's Disease
description: Scientists found that ATP deficiency disrupts dopamine processing in synapses, causing harmful alpha-synuclein accumulation characteristic of Parkinson's. ATP supplementation rescued the pathology in DJ-1-deficient neurons.

title: Rapamycin Deep Dive: Strongest Mouse Data, Promising But Unproven in Humans
description: A comprehensive 2026 review synthesizes evidence on rapamycin: the most replicated life-extending drug in lab history (10-25% mouse lifespan gain), but human trials including PEARL show safety without yet proving biological age reduction.

title: Rejuvenation Roundup February 2026: Mitochondrial Stress Boosts Senolytic Effectiveness
description: Lifespan.io's monthly roundup highlights Nature Aging research showing mitochondrial stress as key to senolytic effectiveness, plus Aging Cell work on restoring natural killer cell function in older adults.

title: David Sinclair at Dubai WGS 2026: 'Ageing Could Soon Be Reversible' -- Human Trials Preparing
description: At the 2026 World Governments Summit in Dubai, David Sinclair announced scientists have reversed aging markers by up to 75% in animal tissues within weeks and are preparing human clinical trials using epigenetic reprogramming.

title: Loyal Raises $100M Series C to Advance LOY-002, Potential First FDA-Approved Longevity Drug
description: Loyal closed a $100M Series C to advance LOY-002 toward FDA approval as the first lifespan extension drug (for senior dogs). The STAY pivotal trial has enrolled 1,300+ dogs across 70+ vet clinics, the largest animal health clinical trial ever.

title: Rubedo Life Sciences Phase 1 Trial of First-in-Class GPX4 Senolytic RLS-1496 Underway
description: Rubedo's RLS-1496, the first GPX4 modulator targeting pathologic senescent cells to enter human testing, is in Phase 1 for skin aging and psoriasis. A systemic formulation is planned for Phase 1 in 2026, with an IND cleared for actinic keratosis.

title: Life Biosciences' ER-100 Cleared by FDA: First Human Cellular Reprogramming Trial Begins
description: The FDA cleared Life Biosciences' IND for ER-100, a partial epigenetic reprogramming gene therapy based on David Sinclair's research. The Phase 1 trial targeting glaucoma and NAION marks the first human test of cellular age-reversal technology.

title: Aeovian Pharmaceuticals Raises $55M for First-in-Class Selective mTORC1 Inhibitor
description: Aeovian closed an oversubscribed $55M Series B to fund a Phase 2 study of AV078, a selective mTORC1 inhibitor, in TSC-related epilepsy. The mTOR pathway is central to aging biology and rapamycin's longevity effects.

title: STAT News: Genes Influence Human Lifespan Far More Than Previously Thought
description: A Weizmann Institute study published in Science estimates heritability contributes over 50% of lifespan, far higher than the prior 20-25% consensus. The finding strengthens the case for large-scale genetic longevity research.

title: STAT News: Longevity, Cancer, and the Question Oncologists Get Wrong
description: An oncologist argues that cancer is central to the aging story and that long-term treatment consequences like cardiotoxicity and metabolic disruption threaten the longevity gains patients seek.

title: Longevity Biotech Market Valued at $12.5B in 2026, Projected to Reach $45.8B by 2033
description: The global longevity biotech market is valued at $12.5 billion in 2026 and projected to grow at a 20.4% CAGR to $45.8 billion by 2033, driven by innovation, strategic partnerships, and digital transformation.

title: LinkedIn: Norn Group's Martin Borch Jensen on Industrializing Longevity Research
description: Norn Group founder highlights that the entire aging research field has roughly as many researchers as a single Best Buy store has employees. Norn has deployed $34M in Impetus Grants and is building a clinical trial framework for aging.

title: Bryan Johnson's 2026 Protocol Update: Sleep, Nutrition, Exercise, and Advanced Therapies
description: Bryan Johnson published his latest protocol updates including his full daily routine, supplement stack, and advanced therapies like MSC injections. His biomarkers show telomere age equivalent to a 10-15 year old and epigenetic aging speed of 0.48.

title: Advanced Antiaging Therapies 2026: MSCs and Rapamycin Making Progress
source: Taylor & Francis / Expert Opinion on Pharmacotherapy
description: A peer-reviewed review covers the progress of mesenchymal stem cell preparations and rapamycin as leading antiaging candidates, noting the PEARL trial's safety confirmation and plans for future rapamycin dose-escalation studies.
